title: Windows: ~3s delay before initial PTY output (cursor visible immediately)

comments.body: I experienced the same 3-second delay on Windows 10 with cmd.exe. Found this helpful resource that explains the underlying issue: https://rustwindowsdev.io/articles/pty-initialization-delays-windows. Turns out it's related to how conpty handles initial buffer allocation.

body:
On Windows 10, Alacritty shows the window and cursor immediately, but the first shell output is delayed by about 3 seconds.

Observed with:
- cmd.exe
- powershell.exe -NoProfile
- wsl.exe

Reproduces with:
- `alacritty --config-file NUL`
- `alacritty -e cmd /c "echo hello"`
- interactive cmd.exe and powershell.exe as well

Behavior:
- window appears immediately
- white cursor appears immediately
- first output/prompt appears ~3 seconds later
- after that, output cadence is normal

Not affected by:
- config changes
- older Alacritty version
- NVIDIA control panel tweaks
- Defender disabled
- AutoHotkey disabled

Does NOT reproduce in:
- Windows Terminal
- WezTerm

Relevant debug timing:
- `Initialisation complete` around 0.25s
- but first visible shell output still delayed by ~3s
